title: Multi-Select Rows for Bulk Editing in DataTables
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to multi-select rows in a data table and apply bulk edits via a dynamic dropdown overlay, so that I can update multiple records efficiently without editing each one individually.
description:
Enhancement to enable multi-row selection with bulk editing capabilities in the DataTables component. When users select multiple rows using Shift+click, a dynamic dropdown overlay appears allowing bulk updates to column values.

## Behavior
- Multi-select rows using Shift+click in the data table
- A dynamic dropdown overlay appears for the relevant editable column
- Selecting a value from the dropdown applies the change to all selected rows and persists to the database
- The per-row inline dropdown is hidden when multiple rows are selected to avoid confusion
- After bulk update, the dynamic dropdown hides automatically

## Scope
- Implemented on both Dictionary tab (reporting field) and Migration tab (migrate column)
- Only available on object detail pages, not the main dashboard
- Database updates are applied in bulk
acceptance_criteria: User can multi-select rows using Shift+click
Dynamic dropdown overlay appears when multiple rows are selected
Bulk edit updates persist to the database for all selected rows
Per-row inline dropdown is hidden during multi-select to avoid confusion
Dynamic dropdown hides after a bulk update is applied
Feature works on both Dictionary and Migration tabs
Feature is only active on object detail pages
ux_changes: Adds multi-row selection via Shift+click with a floating dynamic dropdown overlay for bulk column value editing. Inline per-row dropdowns are hidden during multi-select to prevent confusion.
middleware_changes: Server-side batch update logic processes bulk edits from the multi-select overlay and persists changes to the database for all selected rows.
implementation_notes: A CodePen prototype was created for the multi-select JS logic. Integration required adapting for page-specific elements and ensuring database persistence. Position adjustments were needed since other page elements affected dropdown placement. The feature was extended from Dictionary-only to include the Migration tab's migrate column.
comment_summary: Extensive iterative development. Initial bulk edit worked via popup, then was enhanced to work inline. Developer resolved positioning issues and added database persistence. Feature was extended to Migration tab at Ryan's request. Dynamic dropdown visibility was refined to hide per-row controls during multi-select.
